seoTitle: Orchard Hills vs Orton Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Orchard Hills and Orton Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Orchard Hills vs Orton Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Orchard Hills and Orton Park.
heading: Orchard Hills vs Orton Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Orchard Hills, New South Wales and Orton Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orton Park has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Orchard Hills (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Orchard Hills or Orton Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Orchard Hills has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Orton Park scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Orchard Hills scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Orton Park scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Orchard Hills or Orton Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orton Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
